message:
fix(handlers): cap io.ReadAll on upstream streams (#7963, #7964) (#7987)

Three upstream-body reads were unbounded io.ReadAll calls:

1. benchmarks.go downloadDriveFile read the entire Google Drive response
   without a size limit. A huge or malicious file id could OOM the server.
   Cap at maxBenchmarkReportBytes = 50 MiB (far larger than any real report;
   typical reports are <1 MiB) and surface a real error on truncation
   instead of silently returning a clipped report.

2. kagent_proxy.go CallTool and kagenti_provider_proxy.go CallTool both
   io.ReadAll the agent stream. A runaway tool invocation or adversarial
   agent could wedge the server on memory. Share maxAgentResponseBytes =
   10 MiB across both proxies since they expose the same A2A surface.

All three reads now use io.LimitReader with cap+1 and check the result
length so truncation produces a 502-style error rather than a silent clip.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
